---
title: "Market Comps"
description: "Valuation multiples, operating metrics, and revenue forecasts for every publicly traded Fortune 100 company across seven sectors. Refreshed daily."
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    body-classes: comps-page
    include-after-body: comparables-sort.html
    toc: false
---

Comparable-company analysis is one of the first things an investor or strategist reaches for when sizing up a sector or vetting a name. Market Comps gives you a clean, current set of valuation multiples (EV/EBITDA, P/E, EV/Revenue), operating metrics (margins, FCF yield, leverage), and simple revenue forecasts for every publicly traded Fortune 100 company across seven sectors. Data is pulled from public filings and market feeds, then refreshed daily so the numbers stay current. Click any ticker to open the company on Yahoo Finance.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import os
path = "outputs/comparables/last_updated.txt"
if os.path.isfile(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        date = f.read().strip()
    print(f'<p class="comps-last-updated"><strong>Last updated:</strong> {date}</p>')
else:
    print('<p class="comps-last-updated"><strong>Last updated:</strong> Pending first data refresh.</p>')
```

## By industry

Each table covers one sector. Columns are sortable; click any header to rank by that metric. The final row is the sector median. An <span class="comps-outlier-badge" style="font-size:0.7rem">Outlier</span> flag marks names where a key multiple falls outside 1.5 IQR of the sector.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import os
import glob
import re
import json
import pandas as pd
import html as html_lib

YAHOO_QUOTE = "https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/{}"

COLUMN_LABELS = {
    "ticker": "Ticker",
    "name": "Company",
    "market_cap_m": "Mkt Cap (M)",
    "ev_to_ebitda": "EV / EBITDA",
    "trailing_pe": "Trailing P/E",
    "forward_pe": "Forward P/E",
    "ev_to_revenue": "EV / Revenue",
    "fcf_yield": "FCF Yield",
    "net_debt_ebitda": "Net Debt / EBITDA",
    "profit_margin": "Profit Margin",
    "operating_margin": "Op. Margin",
    "revenue_growth": "Rev. Growth",
    "outlier_flag": "Flag",
}

MULTIPLE_COLS = ["ev_to_ebitda", "trailing_pe", "forward_pe", "ev_to_revenue", "net_debt_ebitda"]

base = "outputs/comparables"
if os.path.isdir(base):
    all_paths = sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(base, "comparables_*.csv")))
    paths = [p for p in all_paths if not re.search(r"_\d{4}-\d{2}\.csv$", p)]

    # Sector navigation
    nav_items = []
    for p in paths:
        s = os.path.basename(p).replace("comparables_", "").replace(".csv", "")
        l = s.replace("_", " ").title()
        nav_items.append(f'<a href="#sector-{s}" class="comps-nav-link">{l}</a>')
    print(f'<nav class="comps-sector-nav">{"".join(nav_items)}</nav>')

    for path in paths:
        name = os.path.basename(path).replace("comparables_", "").replace(".csv", "").replace("_", " ").title()
        safe = os.path.basename(path).replace("comparables_", "").replace(".csv", "")

        # Sector heading
        print(f'<h3 id="sector-{safe}" class="comps-sector-heading">{name}</h3>')

        # Sector pulse (pure HTML — no ::: markdown)
        try:
            pulse_path = os.path.join(base, f"sector_pulse_{safe}.json")
            if os.path.isfile(pulse_path):
                with open(pulse_path) as f:
                    pulse_data = json.load(f)
                pulse_text = pulse_data.get("pulse", "")
                if pulse_text:
                    print(f'<div class="comps-sector-pulse"><strong>Sector pulse:</strong> {pulse_text}</div>')
        except Exception:
            pass

        try:
            df = pd.read_csv(path)
            raw = pd.read_csv(path)

            # Columns to show (no industry, no raw market_cap)
            show = [c for c in ["ticker", "name", "market_cap_m", "ev_to_ebitda", "trailing_pe", "forward_pe", "ev_to_revenue", "fcf_yield", "net_debt_ebitda", "profit_margin", "operating_margin", "revenue_growth", "outlier_flag"] if c in df.columns]
            df = df[show].copy()

            # --- Number formatting ---
            # Market cap
            if "market_cap_m" in df.columns:
                df["market_cap_m"] = df["market_cap_m"].apply(lambda x: f"{x:,.0f}" if pd.notna(x) else "")

            # Multiples: format with x suffix
            for col in MULTIPLE_COLS:
                if col in df.columns:
                    df[col] = df[col].apply(lambda x: f"{float(x):.1f}x" if pd.notna(x) else "" if not isinstance(x, str) else x)

            # Percentages: fcf_yield, revenue_growth
            for col in ("fcf_yield", "revenue_growth"):
                if col in df.columns:
                    def _pct2(x, _col=col, _raw=raw):
                        try:
                            return f"{float(x) * 100:.1f}%" if pd.notna(x) else ""
                        except (TypeError, ValueError):
                            return ""
                    df[col] = df[col].apply(_pct2)

            # Percentages: profit_margin, operating_margin
            for col in ("profit_margin", "operating_margin"):
                if col in df.columns:
                    def _pct(x):
                        try:
                            return f"{float(x) * 100:.1f}%" if pd.notna(x) else ""
                        except (TypeError, ValueError):
                            return ""
                    df[col] = df[col].apply(_pct)

            # Outlier flag as badge
            if "outlier_flag" in df.columns:
                df["outlier_flag"] = df["outlier_flag"].apply(lambda x: '<span class="comps-outlier-badge">Outlier</span>' if x else "")

            # Sector summary card
            n_names = raw["ticker"].nunique() if "ticker" in raw.columns else 0
            med_ev_ebitda = raw["ev_to_ebitda"].median() if "ev_to_ebitda" in raw.columns and pd.notna(raw["ev_to_ebitda"].median()) else None
            med_rev_gr = raw["revenue_growth"].median() if "revenue_growth" in raw.columns and pd.notna(raw["revenue_growth"].median()) else None
            summary_parts = [f"{n_names} names"]
            if med_ev_ebitda is not None:
                summary_parts.append(f"median EV/EBITDA {med_ev_ebitda:.1f}x")
            if med_rev_gr is not None:
                summary_parts.append(f"median rev growth {med_rev_gr*100:.1f}%")
            print(f'<div class="comps-sector-summary">{" &middot; ".join(summary_parts)}</div>')
            print(f'<p><a href="comparables/{os.path.basename(path)}" download class="comps-download-link">&#x2913; Download CSV</a></p>')

            # Clickable ticker and name (Yahoo Finance)
            tickers_raw = raw["ticker"].astype(str) if "ticker" in raw.columns else pd.Series([""] * len(df))
            if "ticker" in df.columns:
                df["ticker"] = [f'<a href="{YAHOO_QUOTE.format(html_lib.escape(t))}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">{html_lib.escape(t)}</a>' for t in tickers_raw]
            if "name" in df.columns:
                names_raw = raw["name"].astype(str) if "name" in raw.columns else df["name"].astype(str)
                df["name"] = [f'<a href="{YAHOO_QUOTE.format(html_lib.escape(t))}" target="_blank" rel="noopener">{html_lib.escape(n)}</a>' for t, n in zip(tickers_raw, names_raw)]

            # Conditional formatting for margins and growth
            med_profit = raw["profit_margin"].median() if "profit_margin" in raw.columns else None
            med_op = raw["operating_margin"].median() if "operating_margin" in raw.columns else None
            med_rev = raw["revenue_growth"].median() if "revenue_growth" in raw.columns else None

            def _cond_fmt(val_str, median_val):
                if not val_str or val_str == "—" or median_val is None or pd.isna(median_val):
                    return val_str
                try:
                    v = float(val_str.replace("%", "")) / 100
                    if v > median_val * 1.0:
                        return f'<span class="comps-val-positive">{val_str}</span>'
                    elif v < median_val * 1.0:
                        return f'<span class="comps-val-negative">{val_str}</span>'
                except (ValueError, TypeError, AttributeError):
                    pass
                return val_str

            if "profit_margin" in df.columns and med_profit is not None:
                df["profit_margin"] = df["profit_margin"].apply(lambda x: _cond_fmt(x, med_profit))
            if "operating_margin" in df.columns and med_op is not None:
                df["operating_margin"] = df["operating_margin"].apply(lambda x: _cond_fmt(x, med_op))
            if "revenue_growth" in df.columns and med_rev is not None:
                df["revenue_growth"] = df["revenue_growth"].apply(lambda x: _cond_fmt(x, med_rev))

            # Sector median row
            median_row = {}
            for c in show:
                if c == "ticker":
                    median_row[c] = "—"
                elif c == "name":
                    median_row[c] = "<strong>Sector median</strong>"
                elif c == "market_cap_m" and "market_cap" in raw.columns:
                    m = raw["market_cap"].median()
                    median_row[c] = f"{m/1e6:,.0f}" if pd.notna(m) else ""
                elif c == "outlier_flag":
                    median_row[c] = ""
                elif c in ("fcf_yield", "revenue_growth") and c in raw.columns:
                    m = raw[c].median()
                    median_row[c] = f"{float(m) * 100:.1f}%" if pd.notna(m) else ""
                elif c in raw.columns and pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(raw[c]):
                    m = raw[c].median()
                    if c in ("profit_margin", "operating_margin"):
                        median_row[c] = f"{float(m) * 100:.1f}%" if pd.notna(m) else ""
                    elif c in MULTIPLE_COLS:
                        median_row[c] = f"{float(m):.1f}x" if pd.notna(m) else ""
                    else:
                        median_row[c] = f"{float(m):,.2f}" if pd.notna(m) else ""
                else:
                    median_row[c] = ""
            df = pd.concat([df, pd.DataFrame([median_row])], ignore_index=True)

            # Replace NaN / empty with em dash
            df = df.fillna("—")
            df = df.replace({"": "—", "nan": "—", "nanx": "—", "NaN": "—"})

            # Rename columns to human-readable labels
            df = df.rename(columns=COLUMN_LABELS)

            html_table = df.to_html(index=False, escape=False, classes="table table-sm comps-table")

            # Mark median row (last <tr>) with CSS class
            last_tr_idx = html_table.rfind('<tr>')
            if last_tr_idx != -1:
                html_table = html_table[:last_tr_idx] + '<tr class="comps-median-row">' + html_table[last_tr_idx + 4:]

            print('<div class="comps-table-wrapper">')
            print(html_table)
            print('</div>')
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"<p>(No data yet. Run the comparables pipeline.)</p>")
else:
    print('<p>Comparables output directory not yet created. Run <code>python run_comparables.py --industry all</code> to generate data.</p>')
```

## Revenue: historical and forecast

We pull quarterly revenue from reported financials and project the next four quarters using a simple growth rate (trailing quarterly growth applied forward). Bear/base/bull bands use 0.5x, 1x, and 1.5x that growth rate. No ML; this is a level 1 forecast for quick comparison. Below: last 2 historical quarters, next 2 forecast quarters, and implied CAGRs (historical vs forecast) for a sanity check.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import os
import glob
import pandas as pd
base = "outputs/comparables"
rev_files = sorted(glob.glob(os.path.join(base, "revenue_forecast_*.csv"))) if os.path.isdir(base) else []
if not rev_files:
    print("<p>No revenue forecast data yet. Run <code>python run_comparables.py --industry all</code> to generate historicals and forecasts.</p>")
else:
    for path in rev_files:
        industry_name = os.path.basename(path).replace("revenue_forecast_", "").replace(".csv", "").replace("_", " ").title()
        print(f'<h3 class="comps-sector-heading">{industry_name}</h3>')
        df = pd.read_csv(path)
        df["period_end"] = pd.to_datetime(df["period_end"])
        df["revenue_b"] = (df["revenue"] / 1e9).round(2)
        # Summary: last 2 historical + next 2 forecast per ticker
        hist = df[df["type"] == "historical"].sort_values(["ticker", "period_end"])
        fcast = df[df["type"] == "forecast"].sort_values(["ticker", "period_end"])
        last2 = hist.groupby("ticker").tail(2)
        next2 = fcast.groupby("ticker").head(2)
        summary = []
        for ticker in df["ticker"].unique():
            h = last2[last2["ticker"] == ticker][["period_end", "revenue_b"]].sort_values("period_end")
            fc = next2[next2["ticker"] == ticker][["period_end", "revenue_b"]].sort_values("period_end")
            row = {"Ticker": ticker, "Hist Q-1": "", "Hist Q-2": "", "Fcst Q+1": "", "Fcst Q+2": ""}
            for i, (_, r) in enumerate(h.tail(2).iterrows()):
                key = "Hist Q-2" if i == 0 else "Hist Q-1"
                row[key] = f"{r['revenue_b']}B"
            for i, (_, r) in enumerate(fc.head(2).iterrows()):
                key = "Fcst Q+1" if i == 0 else "Fcst Q+2"
                row[key] = f"{r['revenue_b']}B"
            summary.append(row)
        tbl = pd.DataFrame(summary)
        print(tbl.to_html(index=False, escape=False, classes="table table-sm comps-table comps-revenue-table"))
        # CAGR: historical (annualized from last 4q) vs forecast (implied from 4q forward)
        hist_df = df[df["type"] == "historical"].sort_values(["ticker", "period_end"])
        fcast_df = df[df["type"] == "forecast"].sort_values(["ticker", "period_end"])
        cagr_rows = []
        for t in df["ticker"].unique()[:10]:
            h = hist_df[hist_df["ticker"] == t]["revenue"]
            fc = fcast_df[fcast_df["ticker"] == t]["revenue"]
            if len(h) >= 2 and len(fc) >= 2:
                n_hist = len(h) - 1
                hist_cagr = (h.iloc[-1] / h.iloc[0]) ** (4 / max(n_hist, 1)) - 1 if n_hist else None
                n_fc = len(fc) - 1
                fcast_cagr = (fc.iloc[-1] / fc.iloc[0]) ** (4 / max(n_fc, 1)) - 1 if n_fc else None
                cagr_rows.append({"Ticker": t, "Historical CAGR": f"{hist_cagr*100:.1f}%" if hist_cagr else "", "Forecast CAGR": f"{fcast_cagr*100:.1f}%" if fcast_cagr else ""})
        if cagr_rows:
            cagr_tbl = pd.DataFrame(cagr_rows)
            print('<p><strong>Implied annualized CAGR (historical vs forecast):</strong></p>')
            print(cagr_tbl.to_html(index=False, escape=False, classes="table table-sm comps-table comps-cagr-table"))
        # Line chart: first 4 tickers, historical + forecast
        try:
            import plotly.graph_objects as go
            from IPython.display import display, HTML
            plot_tickers = df["ticker"].unique()[:4]
            fig = go.Figure()
            for t in plot_tickers:
                for typ, dash, lbl in [("historical", "solid", "hist"), ("forecast", "solid", "fcast"), ("forecast_bear", "dot", "bear"), ("forecast_bull", "dot", "bull")]:
                    sub = df[(df["ticker"] == t) & (df["type"] == typ)]
                    if sub.empty:
                        continue
                    sub = sub.sort_values("period_end")
                    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=sub["period_end"].dt.strftime("%Y-%m"), y=sub["revenue_b"], name=f"{t} {lbl}", line=dict(dash=dash)))
            fig.update_layout(
                title=f"Quarterly Revenue (B USD) — {industry_name}",
                xaxis_title="Quarter",
                yaxis_title="Revenue (B USD)",
                margin=dict(t=50, b=80, l=60, r=20),
                paper_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)",
                plot_bgcolor="rgba(0,0,0,0)",
                font=dict(family="Source Sans Pro, sans-serif", color="#1c2833", size=12),
                xaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e9ecef", linecolor="#2c3e50", linewidth=1),
                yaxis=dict(gridcolor="#e9ecef", linecolor="#2c3e50", linewidth=1),
                legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=-0.3, xanchor="center", x=0.5, font=dict(size=10)),
                colorway=["#1c2833", "#2980b9", "#27ae60", "#e74c3c", "#8e44ad", "#f39c12", "#1abc9c", "#e67e22"],
            )
            display(HTML(fig.to_html(include_plotlyjs="cdn")))
        except Exception:
            pass
```
